senses.definition: the health services and  treatments given to people

examples.x: Access to affordable healthcare is a major concern for many families in the country.
The government introduced new policies to improve the quality of healthcare in rural areas.
She decided to pursue a career in healthcare because she wanted to help others.
The hospital is known for providing excellent healthcare to its patients.
Advances in technology have revolutionized modern healthcare, making treatments more effective and accessible.

senses.definition: the well-being and happiness of an individual or a group
a substance that treats injuries or illnesses

examples.x: The doctor prescribed medicine for her cough.
He took his medicine with a glass of water.
The pharmacist explained how to use the medicine properly.
The medicine needs to be taken twice a day after meals.
The child refused to take the bitter-tasting medicine.

senses.definition: related to medicine, treating illnesses, and health

examples.x: She pursued a career in the medical field to help others improve their health.
His medical knowledge allowed him to provide first aid in emergency situations.
The pharmaceutical company conducts research to develop new medical treatments for diseases.

senses.definition: (of a scientific test) showing that a particular substance or condition exists

examples.x: The positive result of the pregnancy test confirmed her suspicions.
The lab report showed positive findings for the presence of heavy metals in the water supply.
The chemical assay displayed positive levels of glucose in the patient's blood, indicative of diabetes.
The allergen test showed positive reactions to certain substances, confirming allergies.

senses.definition: (of a scientific test) showing that there is no sign of a disease or a particular condition

examples.x: The doctor informed her that the blood test came back negative for any signs of infection.
Despite experiencing symptoms, the X-ray results were negative for any fractures or abnormalities.
After conducting a thorough examination, the veterinarian confirmed that the fecal sample tested negative for parasites in the dog.
The urine sample tested negative for traces of illicit drugs, confirming the individual's claims of sobriety.
The mammogram revealed negative findings, providing reassurance to the patient about her breast health.

senses.definition: any substance that is used for medicinal purposes

examples.x: Drugs prescribed by doctors play a crucial role in treating various medical conditions, from antibiotics for infections to painkillers for managing discomfort.
Herbal remedies, derived from plants, have been used for centuries as natural drugs to address a wide range of health issues, though their efficacy and safety can vary.
The pharmaceutical industry continually researches and develops new drugs to address emerging health challenges and improve patient outcomes.

senses.definition: a type of medicine taken to relieve pain, bring down a fever, etc.

examples.x: She took two aspirin tablets to relieve her headache.
The doctor recommended taking aspirin to reduce the fever.
He keeps a bottle of aspirin in his desk drawer for emergencies.
After spraining his ankle, he took an aspirin to help with the pain.
Aspirin is often used to alleviate the symptoms of the common cold.

senses.definition: a drug that is used to destroy bacteria or stop their growth, like Penicillin
a type of small, rounded drug that has medicine inside, which when swallowed releases its medical substance
a set of tools and medical supplies, usually carried in a bag or case, used in case of emergency or injury

examples.x: Always carry a first-aid kit when hiking in remote areas.
The first-aid kit contains everything needed for minor injuries.
They opened the first-aid kit to tend to the injured player on the field.
The school ensures that every classroom has a stocked first-aid kit.
She kept a first-aid kit in her car for emergencies.

senses.definition: a piece of cloth that is put around a wound to prevent infections

examples.x: She wrapped a bandage around the cut on her finger to protect it from infection.
The nurse applied a sterile bandage to the patient's wound to keep it clean and dry.
In case of an emergency, it’s important to know how to properly use a bandage to dress a wound.
He always kept a first aid kit with extra bandages in his car for any minor injuries while hiking.
After the injury, the doctor instructed him to change the bandage daily to ensure proper healing.

senses.definition: a small sticky strip used to cover and protect an injuriy, typically a cut

examples.x: She reached into her purse and pulled out a Band-Aid, offering it to her coworker who had just gotten a paper cut.

senses.definition: an act of a drug injection to the body

examples.x: He received a shot to manage his pain.
The nurse gave him a shot of insulin.
She was nervous about getting her first shot.
He needed a shot to calm his anxiety during the procedure.
After the shot, he felt a little dizzy.

senses.definition: to lose blood from an injury or wound

examples.x: The paper cut on her finger began to bleed after she accidentally touched it.
When the athlete got a deep cut on his knee, it started to bleed profusely.
It's essential to apply pressure to a wound to stop it from bleeding excessively.
In case of a minor injury, a small scratch may bleed briefly before clotting.
Last week, I accidentally cut my finger, and it bled for a while.

senses.definition: to have an illness or disease

examples.x: The child suffered from a high fever and cough, prompting his parents to take him to the doctor.
As she watched her loved one suffer from cancer, she felt a deep sense of helplessness and despair.
Access to quality healthcare is crucial for those who suffer from chronic illnesses, ensuring timely diagnosis and effective treatment.
The elderly man suffered from arthritis, finding it increasingly challenging to perform simple tasks like tying his shoes.

senses.definition: making one experience pain

examples.x: The painful cut on his hand required immediate attention.
The painful twist of his ankle forced him to stop running.
The painful cut on his finger bled for several minutes.
He received a painful knock on the head during the game.

senses.definition: the process of looking closely at something to identify any issues

examples.x: The mechanic's examination of the engine revealed several problems.
A thorough examination of the data is needed before drawing any conclusions.
The doctor performed an examination to check for any signs of infection.
After a close examination, the architect found structural weaknesses in the building.
The scientist conducted an examination of the samples to detect any contaminants.

senses.definition: a medical examination of the body or a part of body to detect possible health issues

examples.x: She went for a vision test to ensure her eyesight was normal.
He had a series of heart tests to determine the cause of his chest pain.
The test results revealed a deficiency in her vitamin levels.
They performed a biopsy test to examine the tissue for abnormalities.

senses.definition: a medical process in which a part of body is cut open to repair or remove a damaged organ

examples.x: The doctor explained the details of the operation to ensure the patient understood the procedure.
After the operation, the patient was monitored closely in the recovery room for any complications.
He underwent a successful operation to remove the appendix, and he was discharged the following day.
The surgical team performed the operation with precision, resulting in a smooth recovery for the patient.
Prior to the operation, the medical staff conducted several tests to assess the patient’s overall health.

senses.definition: to cut open a part of the body in order to repair or remove a damaged organ

examples.x: The surgeon needed to operate immediately to remove the patient's appendix.

senses.definition: to provide medical care such as medicine or therapy to heal injuries, illnesses, or wounds and make someone better

examples.x: First aid is administered to treat minor injuries and wounds.
Physicians prescribe medications to treat various illnesses and infections.
Instructors often teach individuals how to treat minor injuries.
Last week, the doctor promptly treated the wound to prevent infection.
Dermatologists may recommend creams or ointments to treat skin conditions.

senses.definition: an action that is done to relieve pain or cure a disease, wound, etc.

examples.x: After the accident, he underwent extensive treatment to recover from his injuries and regain mobility.
The doctor prescribed a course of antibiotics as part of the treatment for the bacterial infection.
Physical therapy was a crucial aspect of her treatment plan to rehabilitate her injured knee.
The holistic approach to cancer treatment included chemotherapy, radiation therapy, and complementary therapies to support overall well-being.
Timely treatment of acute illnesses can prevent complications and facilitate a quicker recovery process.

senses.definition: to make someone regain their health

examples.x: Antibiotics are often prescribed to cure bacterial infections.
Medical advancements have led to the development of vaccines that can cure certain diseases.
Proper medication and lifestyle changes can help cure certain health conditions.
Over the years, medical breakthroughs have successfully cured various diseases.
If the clinical trial is successful, the treatment will likely cure the disease.

senses.definition: to become healthy again

examples.x: Over time, a wound may heal and gradually close.
Patients receive medical care to help injuries heal properly.
The injury is currently healing with the prescribed treatment.
Many individuals are actively healing with the help of therapy.
Patients have recently healed after undergoing medical procedures.

senses.definition: relating to the health or state of the mind, including aspects of emotional, psychological, and cognitive well-being

examples.x: She practices meditation to improve her mental well-being and reduce stress.
Mental clarity is essential for making important decisions under pressure.

senses.definition: to extend or increase in influence or effect over a larger area or group of people

examples.x: The flu virus can easily spread in crowded places.
Right now, concerns about the pandemic are spreading across the globe.
The excitement of the victory spread to the fans.
The new trend spread rapidly among young people.
His passion for the project spread to the entire department.
The revolution spread quickly across neighboring countries.
Yesterday, the wildfire rapidly spread through the dry forest.
As the drought worsened, the scarcity of water spread throughout the region.
The use of radios spread to remote areas, allowing people to receive news faster.

senses.definition: a change in the normal condition of the body of a person, which is the sign of a disease

examples.x: A persistent cough can be a symptom of a respiratory infection.
The patient reported a fever as one of the first symptoms of the flu.
Muscle aches and fatigue are common symptoms of many viral infections.
The rash on his skin was a clear symptom of an allergic reaction.
She visited the doctor because of severe headaches, a symptom she couldn't ignore.

senses.definition: to regain complete health after a period of sickness or injury

examples.x: After a week of rest, she started to recover from the flu.
Physical therapy helped him recover from a sports injury.
The patient is currently recovering after the surgery.
Last month, she promptly recovered from the illness.
With proper treatment, many people can recover from mental health challenges.

senses.definition: the process of becoming healthy again after an injury or disease
the written instructions of a doctor that allow the patient to get the medicines needed
the act of providing treatment and paying attention to the physical and emotional needs of someone or something

examples.x: The elderly residents of the nursing home received compassionate care from the dedicated staff members.
His love for animals led him to pursue a career in veterinary care, where he could help animals in need.
The hospital provides compassionate care to all patients, ensuring their physical and emotional needs are met.

senses.definition: to provide treatment for or help a person or an animal that is sick or injured

examples.x: The nurse cares for elderly patients at the hospital.
The veterinarian will care for your dog during its recovery.
The rescue team cares for injured wildlife before releasing them back into the wild.
She stayed home to care for her sick mother.
The nurse carefully cared for the elderly patient in the hospital.

senses.definition: a basic medical treatment given to someone in an emergency before they are taken to the hospital
a complete medical examination of the body to see if there are any health issues

examples.x: She scheduled her annual checkup to ensure she was in good health.
The doctor recommended a checkup to catch any early signs of illness.
It's important to have a regular checkup to maintain optimal health.
He went for a checkup after feeling unwell for several weeks.
During the checkup, the physician conducted various tests to evaluate her health.

senses.definition: to have a specific weight

examples.x: The baby elephant weighs over 200 pounds.
The parcel weighs five kilograms, making it a heavy shipment.

senses.definition: a deadly substance that can kill or seriously harm if it enters the body

examples.x: The scientist studied the effects of poison on the nervous system.
She accidentally ingested poison after mistaking it for medication.
The forensic team analyzed the victim's blood for traces of poison.
Environmental pollution can act as a form of poison to ecosystems.
The bottle was clearly labeled as containing a dangerous poison.

senses.definition: to get sick with a virus that causes a runny nose, cough, and sore throat

examples.x: I felt terrible yesterday; I caught cold after getting soaked in the rain.
She caught a cold after being out in the rain.
I hope I don't catch a cold from my coworker who's sneezing.
